title: onobrychis aucheri subsp. psammophila
intro: Onobrychis aucheri subsp. psammophila, also known as Psammophila or Sand Bird's-foot Trefoil, is a plant species belonging to the legume family and is native to the Middle East and North Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, and has been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Onobrychis aucheri subsp. psammophila include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and optimal dosages.
